# Discord Channel Analysis: 💬-discussion

## 1. Summary

The discussion primarily focused on token-related confusion and technical comparisons. The main technical clarification involved the distinction between $ai6z and $elizaos tokens - initially users confirmed they were the same, but Odilitime corrected that they are different contracts with ai16z worth significantly less. Multiple users requested the ElizaOS contract address, with MDMnvest directing them to the appropriate channel.

A key technical comparison emerged when C0rrupt1 asked about differences from OpenClaw. Odilitime provided specific technical advantages: ElizaOS performs better on benchmarks, has more connectors, and is more reliable due to not being self-configuring. This represents a concrete technical positioning statement.

Community concerns centered on token migration deadlines, the rebrand from ai16z to ElizaOS, and perceived lack of marketing follow-through. DorianD noted the rebrand lost memetic value by switching to a "non-memetic ticker." Market performance discussions highlighted the broader crypto winter context, with most assets down 99%.

Odilitime promoted their Babylon Discord demo called "the hanging plaza" as the best current demonstration of agents in action. ElizaBAO announced ElizaOS received a golden checkmark verification. The Milady team submitted a hackathon entry on BSC that was shared for voting.

## 2. FAQ

Q: Is $ai6z are $elizaos the same token? (asked by Drizello) A: Initially confirmed yes by Maxx Truant and Farzeen arif, but Odilitime corrected that they are different contracts with ai16z worth way less (answered by Odilitime)

Q: Why Ai16z coin is pumping? I thought the new migrated coin is ElizaOs now (asked by EnC) A: Unanswered

Q: Can i have contract adress of elizaos (asked by Yoree) A: Directed to check the appropriate channel (answered by MDMnvest)

Q: Why the token is mintable (asked by Yoree) A: Unanswered

Q: I missed the migration date for my ai16z, is there anyway to still do this? (asked by Jüju) A: Unanswered

Q: What happened to all the promo and marketing promised after autofun flopped? Where's Elizacloud? (asked by Thanos💨) A: Unanswered

Q: How is this different from openclaw? (asked by C0rrupt1) A: ElizaOS performs better on benchmarks, has more connectors and is more reliable since it's not self-configuring (answered by Odilitime)

Q: Is the Cultanime team doing well? (asked by Odung) A: They're doing very well (answered by Odilitime)

## 3. Help Interactions

Helper: Maxx Truant, Farzeen arif | Helpee: Drizello | Context: Confusion about whether $ai6z and $elizaos are the same token | Resolution: Initially confirmed they were the same (later corrected by Odilitime)

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: Drizello, EnC | Context: Clarifying token contract differences | Resolution: Explained ai16z and elizaos are different contracts with different values

Helper: MDMnvest | Helpee: Yoree | Context: Requesting ElizaOS contract address | Resolution: Directed to check the appropriate channel for contract information

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: Quaser M | Context: Looking for information about Shaw's statement | Resolution: Provided link to the relevant channel and fixupx post

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: AIKO444 | Context: New user wanting to learn about agents in human digital spaces | Resolution: Welcomed and directed to Babylon discord demo "the hanging plaza"

Helper: MDMnvest | Helpee: NintyNine | Context: Looking for Twitter handles for ElizaOK and Milaidi | Resolution: Added both to the appropriate channel with instructions to find official socials via dexscreener

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: C0rrupt1 | Context: Understanding differences between ElizaOS and OpenClaw | Resolution: Explained technical advantages including better benchmarks, more connectors, and reliability

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: Odung | Context: Concerned about Cultanime team status | Resolution: Confirmed they're doing very well

## 4. Action Items

Type: Documentation | Description: Update contract address information in the designated channel for easier access | Mentioned By: MDMnvest

Type: Feature | Description: Address token migration process for users who missed the deadline | Mentioned By: Jüju

Type: Technical | Description: Clarify token mintability concerns for ElizaOS | Mentioned By: Yoree
Weak communications causing unnecessary negative sentiment from influential groups

# Discord Channel Analysis: 💬-coders

## 1. Summary

The chat segment contains minimal technical discussion, primarily consisting of casual conversation and bug reporting for ElizaCloud. DorianD conducted informal testing of the ElizaCloud dashboard and API explorer, identifying several UI/UX issues. The main technical problems reported include: broken scrolling functionality on the API explorer page (https://www.elizacloud.ai/dashboard/api-explorer) requiring manual use of a hard-to-see scrollbar, similar mousepad scrolling issues on other pages, and a non-functional "send request" test feature that returns an "api key is required" error despite an API key appearing to be present. DorianD also raised questions about pricing transparency and the "use a different key" feature, questioning whether it supports BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) similar to OpenRouter. Odilitime acknowledged the bug reports and forwarded them to the development team. The conversation also included two off-topic messages from KingRon and Wes about non-technical matters. No code implementations, architectural decisions, or problem-solving solutions were discussed in this segment.

## 2. FAQ

Q: Is there still some live Eliza agent that can answer questions about the most recent ElizaOS codebase? (asked by DorianD) A: Unanswered

Q: What's the best way to report bugs for Cloud? (asked by DorianD) A: Odilitime forwarded the reported issues to the team (answered by Odilitime)

Q: Does "use a different key" mean that if user picked gpt-4o that we can enter in an OpenAI key similar to OpenRouter BYOK? (asked by DorianD) A: Unanswered

## 3. Help Interactions

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: DorianD | Context: Bug reporting process for ElizaCloud scrolling issues | Resolution: Forwarded the bug reports to the development team

## 4. Action Items

Type: Technical | Description: Fix scrolling functionality on ElizaCloud API explorer page - mousepad scrolling broken, scrollbar hard to see | Mentioned By: DorianD

Type: Technical | Description: Fix "send request" test feature returning "api key is required" error when API key appears to be present | Mentioned By: DorianD

Type: Documentation | Description: Add price breakdown per model in ElizaCloud dashboard | Mentioned By: DorianD

Type: Documentation | Description: Clarify functionality of "use a different key" feature and whether it supports BYOK | Mentioned By: DorianD

# Discord Channel Analysis: 💬-discussion

## 1. Summary

The discussion channel showed minimal technical content, primarily consisting of community management questions and general conversation. 

The most substantive technical exchange involved **Odilitime** providing project updates, confirming that Babylon is now open to the top 5000 users on the waitlist. This was in response to community concerns about product delivery timelines following the Autofun incident and Twitter issues with Eliza labs.

**k1kkoS** inquired about contacting Shaw, with **Odilitime** suggesting using @ mentions as the standard approach. Odilitime also clarified Shaw's communication strategy: AI/dev content is posted on Twitter while crypto-related updates are shared on Farcaster, requiring followers to monitor both platforms.

**alex_wukar** asked twice about Milady token validity on BNB and Solana chains but received no response.

Two developers (**! Alex !** and **Donhill**) posted availability for project work. ! Alex ! offered general development services, while Donhill specifically mentioned expertise in Web3 infrastructure, backend services, security checks, and UX design. **Boj/acc** sarcastically responded that there were no projects, though this appears to be humor rather than factual.

**Odilitime** conducted an informal poll asking how many users still need to migrate (context unclear from transcript).

The channel contained significant off-topic content including introductions, bot spam complaints, and heated community debate about project leadership that didn't yield technical outcomes.

## 2. FAQ

Q: Is there a way to get in contact with Shaw here? (asked by k1kkoS) A: You can try and @ him, that's what others do (answered by Odilitime)

Q: For Milady, we have BNB and Solana. Both are valid? (asked by alex_wukar) A: Unanswered

Q: Does anyone have a project idea or an active project in progress? (asked by ! Alex !) A: No projects here, you must be in the wrong place (answered by Boj/acc)

Q: How many here still need to migrate? (asked by Odilitime) A: Unanswered

Q: Is anyone building Web3 infra and needs a dev for backend services, security checks, and clean UX? (asked by Donhill) A: Unanswered

## 3. Help Interactions

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: k1kkoS | Context: Needed to contact Shaw | Resolution: Suggested using @ mentions as the standard method

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: Community (Thanos💨) | Context: Concerns about product delivery and updates | Resolution: Clarified that Babylon is now open to top 5000 on waitlist and Shaw provides updates on Twitter

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: Community | Context: Confusion about where to find Shaw's updates | Resolution: Explained Shaw posts AI/dev content on Twitter and crypto content on Farcaster

## 4. Action Items

Type: Documentation | Description: Need clarity on Milady token validity across BNB and Solana chains | Mentioned By: alex_wukar

Type: Technical | Description: Complete migration process for remaining users | Mentioned By: Odilitime

# Discord Channel Analysis: 💬-discussion

## 1. Summary

The discussion centered on ElizaOS technical setup and project positioning. **BinaryCookies** sought guidance on version stability and database configuration. **Odilitime** recommended the v2.0.0 branch despite less documentation, as it represents the project's future direction. For database setup, Odilitime explained that plugin-sql supports both pglite and Neon Postgres, using environment variables to determine which to use, though data migration between them isn't automatic.

**King Nebuluz** shared work on building unbreakable agents (github.com/nebuluzno/scr) using Erlang, offering the code for ElizaOS integration. Odilitime reviewed it positively, noting it was better than previous Erlang projects examined. King Nebuluz is developing a Polymarket agent before returning to contribute to ElizaOS.

**DorianD** reported an unusual bug where their openclaw agent started responding in Korean unexpectedly.

Significant project clarification occurred when **PKScouser** inquired about investment rationale. Odilitime provided extensive context: ElizaOS powers many crypto agents with battle-tested open source code. The project migrated from ai16z to elizaOS (1:6 ratio, with 1:4 for funding), expanding from Solana-only to multi-chain. **Spartan** is the agentic wallet with trading capabilities, accessible to DegenAI holders. Odilitime emphasized the project's mission is democratizing AI agent capabilities for everyday users versus big companies, not short-term profits.

**digitalalchemy** predicted clawd bot will prove trustless while Eliza becomes "Trusted Agentic Intelligence (TAI)" within months. The community discussed challenges of building proactive viral agents while maintaining security through sandboxing.

## 2. FAQ

Q: Which version of ElizaOS is most stable currently? (asked by BinaryCookies) A: v2.0.0 branch is recommended, though it has less documentation but represents the project's direction (answered by Odilitime)

Q: How can I use both pglite and Neon cloud database together? (asked by BinaryCookies) A: plugin-sql supports both and uses the postgres credentials env var to decide which to use, but won't migrate data automatically (answered by Odilitime)

Q: Why should I invest in elizaOS? (asked by PKScouser) A: If you want short-term money, probably shouldn't. If you want to support open source builders ensuring everyday people have same capabilities as big companies, this is the place. Code powers many crypto agents with battle-tested open source (answered by Odilitime)

Q: What is Spartan? (asked by PKScouser) A: Spartan is the agentic wallet with trading capabilities. DegenAI holders have access to the trader which is open source - a potluck of trading bot combined with LLM intelligence (answered by Odilitime)

Q: Is ai16z still tradeable or only eliza? (asked by PKScouser) A: ai16z is a dead coin for all intents and purposes. Migrated to elizaOS with 1:6 ratio, using 1:4 for funding and splitting liquidity across multiple chains (answered by Odilitime)

Q: Is elizaOS a meme coin? (asked by PKScouser) A: Not directly answered, but context suggests it's positioned as a utility/infrastructure token rather than meme coin (answered by Odilitime)

Q: Do you expect DegenAI to do better than ElizaOS token? (asked by BhnInvestor) A: Hired into team degen and felt better to bet on myself/my team. Sold entire elizaOS bags and bought back, but never sold degen bags (answered by Odilitime)

## 3. Help Interactions

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: BinaryCookies | Context: New builder needed guidance on which ElizaOS version to use | Resolution: Recommended v2.0.0 branch as the future direction despite less documentation

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: BinaryCookies | Context: Needed to configure both pglite and Neon Postgres databases | Resolution: Explained plugin-sql supports both via env vars, though data won't auto-migrate

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: PKScouser | Context: New community member seeking understanding of ElizaOS value proposition and project structure | Resolution: Provided comprehensive explanation of project mission, Spartan wallet, migration from ai16z, and positioning as open source infrastructure

Helper: Skinny | Helpee: PKScouser | Context: Seeking investment thesis from Discord | Resolution: Advised against sourcing investment thesis from Discord, recommended building an agent to gain insights

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: King Nebuluz | Context: Shared Erlang-based unbreakable agents code for potential ElizaOS integration | Resolution: Reviewed the code positively, noted it was better than previous Erlang projects

## 4. Action Items

Type: Technical | Description: Review and potentially integrate github.com/nebuluzno/scr unbreakable agents code into ElizaOS | Mentioned By: King Nebuluz

Type: Technical | Description: Investigate openclaw agent unexpectedly writing in Korean | Mentioned By: DorianD

Type: Technical | Description: Complete Polymarket agent development | Mentioned By: King Nebuluz

Type: Documentation | Description: Improve documentation for v2.0.0 branch as it becomes the recommended version | Mentioned By: Odilitime

Type: Feature | Description: Develop clawd bot as trustless system while Eliza becomes Trusted Agentic Intelligence (TAI) | Mentioned By: digitalalchemy

# Discord Channel Analysis: 🥇-partners

## 1. Summary

This brief chat segment primarily focused on two topics: elizaOS token holder benefits and the milady.ai token launch status.

Odilitime shared news that elizaOS holders will receive an upgrade, linking to a related announcement. DannyNOR NoFapArc inquired about purchasing the milady.ai token through Phantom wallet but couldn't locate it. Odilitime clarified that the token hasn't launched yet and committed to following up for more information.

The conversation also touched on creative collaboration strategy. Odilitime mentioned the team is expanding partnerships with external artists who share similar values and goals, moving beyond just internal labs talent. This approach was described as successful ("paying off"). The recovery of X (Twitter) accounts was noted as beneficial for these collaborative efforts.

No technical implementations, code discussions, or specific problem-solving occurred in this segment. The chat was primarily informational updates and brief community questions.

## 2. FAQ

Q: Where do we buy the milady.ai token, can't find it in phantom? (asked by DannyNOR NoFapArc) A: I don't think it's launched yet, I'll ask (answered by Odilitime)

## 3. Help Interactions

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: DannyNOR NoFapArc | Context: Unable to find milady.ai token in Phantom wallet | Resolution: Clarified token hasn't launched yet and offered to investigate further

## 4. Action Items

# Discord Channel Analysis: 💬-coders

## 1. Summary

This chat segment contains minimal technical discussion, primarily consisting of announcements and brief observations. The most significant technical contribution came from Ogie, who announced the publication of a SolCex Exchange plugin for elizaOS (v2.0.0). This plugin, available as @buzzbd/plugin-solcex-bd on npm, functions as an autonomous business development agent specifically designed for cryptocurrency exchange listings. The plugin integrates 8 services, implements a 100-point token scoring system, and aggregates data from 16 intelligence sources. Ogie submitted a registry pull request (#263) and referenced ERC-8004 implementations on both Base (#17483) and Ethereum (#25045) networks. The developer expressed interest in collaborating with builders working on Jeju or agent commerce projects.

Jin made a brief observation about the elizaOS news RSS feed, sharing the feed URL (https://elizaos.news/rss/feed.xml). Odilitime provided a minor correction regarding the project naming convention, clarifying that the correct capitalization is "elizaOS" rather than "ElizaOS." Matt requested assistance with opening a ticket but received no visible response in this segment.

The chat shows limited collaborative problem-solving or in-depth technical discussions during this timeframe.

## 2. FAQ

Q: Can I get assistance with opening a ticket? (asked by Matt - Timpi) A: Unanswered

## 3. Help Interactions

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: jin | Context: Incorrect capitalization of project name | Resolution: Corrected naming convention from "ElizaOS" to "elizaOS"

## 4. Action Items

Type: Feature | Description: Review and merge registry PR #263 for SolCex Exchange plugin | Mentioned By: Ogie
Type: Technical | Description: Connect with builders working on Jeju/agent commerce for potential collaboration on SolCex plugin | Mentioned By: Ogie
Type: Technical | Description: Investigate ticket opening assistance request | Mentioned By: Matt - Timpi

# Discord Chat Analysis - core-devs Channel

## 1. Summary

The discussion centered around PR #6521 for elizaOS/eliza, which involves database refactoring and migration strategy changes for version 2.0.0. Stan submitted a PR with significant code deletion (-2600 lines) primarily removing auto-migration functionality from version 1.4.x to 1.6.x, as the team decided this migration path is no longer necessary for v2.0.0. The rationale is that users are assumed to not be on 1.4.x anymore, with a potential future implementation to throw an error if direct migration from 1.4.x to v2.0.0 is attempted, directing users to migrate to 1.6.x first.

Odilitime mentioned working on a separate "great db refactor PR" and expressed interest in integrating Stan's changes, noting he had ensured Row Level Security (RLS) was maintained in his refactor. Stan confirmed the changes are minimal and easy to rebase, making integration straightforward.

A brief discussion occurred about HTTP server implementation wrapping runtime in V2.0.0. Stan inquired if Odilitime had added this functionality, which Odilitime denied, but Stan later confirmed that Milady has this implementation along with numerous examples in the 2.0.0 version.

Odilitime reported encountering 200k token limits regularly due to numerous plugins, identifying bootstrap providers and evaluations as primary contributors to the issue. He is actively working on improvements to address these baseline performance concerns.

## 2. FAQ

Q: Why was the auto migration from 1.4.x to 1.6.x removed in v2? (asked by Odilitime) A: Because the team assumes users are no longer on version 1.4.x, and they could potentially throw an error asking users to migrate to 1.6.x first before going to v2.0.0 (answered by Stan ⚡)

Q: Did you add a small HTTP server that wraps runtime on V2.0.0? (asked by Stan ⚡) A: No, but Milady has it and there are examples in 2.0.0 (answered by Odilitime)

## 3. Help Interactions

Helper: Stan ⚡ | Helpee: Odilitime | Context: Concerns about rebasing changes from PR #6521 with the db refactor PR | Resolution: Stan confirmed the changes are small and very easy to rebase, with the big deletion being migration-related code that's no longer needed

Helper: Stan ⚡ | Helpee: Odilitime | Context: Understanding why auto-migration was removed | Resolution: Explained the migration strategy change and suggested potential error handling for direct 1.4.x to v2.0.0 upgrades

## 4. Action Items

Type: Technical | Description: Rebase and deconflict PR #6521 with the db refactor PR | Mentioned By: Odilitime

Type: Technical | Description: Consider implementing error handling to prevent direct migration from 1.4.x to v2.0.0, directing users to 1.6.x first | Mentioned By: Stan ⚡

Type: Technical | Description: Improve bootstrap providers and evaluations to address 200k token limit issues caused by numerous plugins | Mentioned By: Odilitime

# Discord Chat Analysis for 💬-discussion

## 1. Summary

The discussion centered around ElizaOS development capabilities and ecosystem updates. DarmaStef showcased experience building AI agents using ElizaOS, including Reservation Agent, Travel Agent, and Database analysis Agent for non-technical users, offering to collaborate on new projects.

A significant announcement came from Odilitime regarding an upgrade for ElizaOS holders, referencing a tweet from shawmakesmagic. The conversation touched on market capitalization history, with the token previously reaching almost 3B according to DannyNOR NoFapArc.

Technical discussions focused on NFT capabilities and infrastructure. Odilitime mentioned ongoing plugin-evm work that will unlock more NFT functionality, acknowledging current limitations. A question arose about Spartan infrastructure routing Milady wallet orders. Odilitime clarified that while Milady wallet might use similar tech/plugins as Spartan, it's not directly related to their implementation.

There was confusion about a Milady BSC announcement, with user 'g' questioning whether an upgrade was for the wrong token ($milady.ai vs ElizaOS). The team's activity status was questioned by albi06856, with Odilitime confirming continued active development.

## 2. FAQ

Q: Can you create any AI agent with any capabilities via ElizaOS? (asked by Omid Sa) A: Implied yes, as DarmaStef demonstrated building various agent types (answered by Omid Sa)

Q: What was the top market cap of this? (asked by Alexei) A: Almost 3B (answered by DannyNOR NoFapArc)

Q: Is this team still active? (asked by albi06856) A: Yes sir (answered by Odilitime)

Q: Could this run again? (asked by albi06856) A: Unanswered

Q: Is there an Eliza NFT? (asked by brickolascage) A: Maybe they'll do an elizaOK one or something (answered by Odilitime)

Q: Is Spartan set up to route and execute Milady wallet orders? (asked by Skinny) A: Might be using the same tech/plugins, so it's spartan tech but not really related to our dude (answered by Odilitime)

Q: Can price be discussed here? (asked by al.mark) A: Discuss in the trading channel (answered by MDMnvest)

Q: Isn't the upgrade for $milady.ai not ElizaOS? (asked by g) A: Unanswered

## 3. Help Interactions

Helper: Omid Sa | Helpee: General audience | Context: Demonstrating ElizaOS capabilities | Resolution: Suggested asking DarmaStef to create AI agents with any capabilities via ElizaOS

Helper: DannyNOR NoFapArc | Helpee: Alexei | Context: Question about historical market cap | Resolution: Provided specific figure of almost 3B

Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: Skinny | Context: Confusion about Spartan and Milady wallet infrastructure relationship | Resolution: Clarified they may use same tech/plugins but aren't directly related

Helper: MDMnvest | Helpee: al.mark | Context: Uncertainty about where to discuss price | Resolution: Directed to appropriate trading channel

Helper: Lexpo4777 | Helpee: 好人有好报 | Context: Personal family health crisis | Resolution: Offered prayers and support

## 4. Action Items

Type: Technical | Description: Continue plugin-evm work to unlock more NFT functionality | Mentioned By: Odilitime

# Discord Chat Analysis - core-devs Channel

## 1. Summary

The discussion began with technical optimization work around MCP loading and METATOOL search actions. Stan ⚡ suggested moving their solution into the framework. Odilitime identified that token usage issues stem primarily from recent messages and reflections collecting too much data, not from actions. He noted the metatool pattern could apply to providers but questioned if the delay would be worthwhile.

The conversation shifted dramatically when 's' announced major organizational changes driven by financial constraints. The team is being trimmed to reduce overhead and focus on immediate revenue generation. The company is pivoting to a venture model with small, focused teams working on fixed budgets rather than traditional daily standups.

Key revenue-focused projects launching immediately include:
- Agent Arena Duel Betting in Hyperscape (2% + 1% fees, launching next week)
- OTC Agent at tradingdesk.fun (1% fee, maintained by 's')
- Milady (free/open source, driving users to Eliza Cloud for main revenue)
- Babylon (crypto trading platform with 1% fees, ramping from 1% to 100% users)
- Eliza App (same backend as hosted Milady, needs onboarding to start charging)
- Amelia's subscription-focused project

The company has received acquisition offers but 's' prefers to achieve profitability first for better valuation. The strategy emphasizes shipping working products immediately, avoiding side quests, and validating projects early with quick kill decisions for non-performers. 's' will focus on Milady until shipped, then Babylon/Hyperscape integration, then Jeju (a network for agents). Shadow from Eliza Wakes Up is collaborating on backend work for both Eliza app and Milady, offering "workflow" and "full sandbox" agent options.

## 2. FAQ

Q: Could the metatool pattern work with providers? (asked by Odilitime) A: Possible but the delay might not be worth it, may need to implement eventually (answered by Odilitime)

Q: What are the budgets per project? (asked by Odilitime) A: Unanswered

Q: Is v2.0.0 still important given it's not listed as a priority project? (asked by Odilitime) A: Unanswered

Q: Are you and Shadow taking over cloud or what exactly? (asked by Odilitime) A: Unanswered

## 3. Help Interactions

Helper: Stan ⚡ | Helpee: Odilitime | Context: Token usage optimization with MCP loading and METATOOL search actions | Resolution: Offered to share their work and potentially move solution into framework, though Odilitime identified his issue was with messages/reflections not actions

## 4. Action Items

Type: Technical | Description: Launch Agent Arena Duel Betting in Hyperscape v1 with 2% + 1% fees next week | Mentioned By: s

Type: Technical | Description: Ramp Babylon from 1% to 100% user rollout and build crypto trading platform version | Mentioned By: s

Type: Technical | Description: Complete Eliza App onboarding flow and billing to start charging customers | Mentioned By: s

Type: Technical | Description: Ship Milady to drive users to Eliza Cloud for revenue | Mentioned By: s

Type: Technical | Description: Integrate Eliza app with Babylon and Hyperscape after Milady ships | Mentioned By: s

Type: Technical | Description: Build Jeju network for agents | Mentioned By: s

Type: Technical | Description: Complete backend work with Shadow for workflow and full sandbox agent options | Mentioned By: s

Type: Feature | Description: Consider moving MCP loading and METATOOL search solution into framework | Mentioned By: Stan ⚡

# Discord Channel Analysis: 💬-discussion

## 1. Summary

The discussion channel contained minimal substantive technical content. The primary technical interaction involved BinaryCookies seeking guidance on image generation plugins for agents. They inquired about the plugin-image-generation package created a year ago and whether better alternatives existed. # Analysis of xfn-framework Discord Chat

## 1. Summary

The discussion centered around Nous Research's newly released Hermes Agent framework. Odilitime analyzed the repository, noting it's 75% Python and appears to be a combination of "zoey and openclaw" components. The only feature that caught interest was "trajectory compression for fitting training data into token budgets." The framework was primarily developed by Teknium without contributions from Roperito, contrary to initial speculation about Life Engine overlap.

A critical version control issue emerged regarding the develop branch unexpectedly containing 2.0.0 code. Odilitime investigated but couldn't trace the source through PRs or commits, describing the situation as unfixable through normal means. The decision was made to create a `v2-develop` branch to preserve 1.x code for users in transition.

An additional issue surfaced regarding GitHub issues synchronization with Linear, creating what Stan described as a "mess" requiring cleanup. The bidirectional sync had caused problems with issue tracking across both platforms.

Q: Was Linear synced with GitHub for issues? (asked by Odilitime) A: Yes, both sides were synced (answered by Stan ⚡)
